title: Department Protocol Supersedes Prior Authorization
scenario: A clinical action is authorized under a general policy, but a department-specific protocol supersedes it before execution.
initial_state: The action was authorized under the general policy.
state_transition: A department-specific protocol becomes controlling before execution.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated under the current department protocol.
constitutional_evaluation: Execution must conform to the controlling policy applicable at the execution boundary.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the controlling department protocol no longer admits the action.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when a controlling protocol supersedes prior authorization.
clinical_action: Execute action under superseded general policy
